headline: Codex introduces flexible pricing for enterprise teams, enhancing accessibility and scalability.

items.title: Codex now offers more flexible pricing for teams
items.summary: OpenAI's Codex has introduced a new pay-as-you-go pricing model for its ChatGPT Business and Enterprise users. This update provides teams with a more adaptable and scalable approach to adopting Codex, making it easier for companies to integrate AI into their workflows without upfront commitments. The flexible pricing is aimed at increasing accessibility and encouraging broader adoption of Codex’s capabilities across various industries. This change reflects OpenAI's strategy to cater to diverse business needs and optimize resource allocation.
items.why_it_matters: Tech leads can leverage this flexible pricing to experiment with AI integrations without significant financial commitments.

items.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
items.summary: Google has introduced new features in the Gemini API, known as 'Dials,' allowing developers to optimize cost and reliability during inference. These options enable developers to choose between faster processing times or cost efficiency, tailoring the API usage to specific project needs. Such flexibility is crucial for developers managing large-scale AI deployments, where resource allocation can significantly impact performance and budget.
items.why_it_matters: This update offers tech leads more control over API costs and performance, facilitating better budget management and project scalability.

items.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate AI agents in Strands Evals
items.summary: AWS has released ActorSimulator within the Strands Evaluations SDK, a tool designed to simulate realistic user interactions for AI agents. This feature allows developers to integrate structured user simulations into their evaluation pipelines, enhancing the accuracy and reliability of multi-turn agent evaluations. The tool aims to improve the development and fine-tuning of AI systems by providing a more robust testing environment.

items.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
items.summary: Google DeepMind has unveiled Gemma 4, their latest open model series focused on advanced reasoning and agent workflows. These models are designed to enhance agentic workflows, offering improvements in processing capabilities and efficiency. Gemma 4 represents a significant leap from its predecessors, emphasizing open access and applicability in various AI-driven tasks.

items.title: Run Linux containers on Android, no root required
items.summary: Podroid, an open-source project, now allows developers to run Linux containers on Android devices without requiring root access. This capability opens new possibilities for leveraging Android devices as development platforms and testing environments, particularly for AI applications that benefit from containerization.

items.title: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
items.summary: Anthropic has announced that starting April 4, users will no longer be able to use Claude Code subscription limits for third-party harnesses, including OpenClaw. This change will require users to adhere strictly to usage within the Claude platform, potentially impacting developers relying on third-party integrations.

items.title: Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
items.summary: Anthropic's latest research explores how emotion concepts function within large language models, providing insights into the cognitive processes underpinning AI text generation. This research could influence how emotion is modeled in AI, enhancing the development of more nuanced and human-like interactions.

items.title: Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise
items.summary: A recent security incident involving the axios NPM package highlights vulnerabilities in the supply chain, leading to a detailed post-mortem analysis by the development community. This incident underscores the importance of robust security practices in open-source projects.

items.title: We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant
items.summary: Mintlify shares their experience of enhancing their AI documentation assistant by replacing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a virtual filesystem. This change has improved performance and reliability, showcasing an innovative approach to AI documentation.

all_items.summary: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.

all_items.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
all_items.summary: This post describes how TGS achieved near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanded context windows for their Vision Transformer-based SFM using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod. This joint solution cut training time from 6 months to just 5 days while enabling analysis of seismic volumes larger than previously possible.

all_items.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
all_items.summary: In this post, we show you how to configure AWS Network Firewall to restrict AgentCore resources to an allowlist of approved internet domains. This post focuses on domain-level filtering using SNI inspection — the first layer of a defense-in-depth approach.

all_items.title: Rocket Close transforms mortgage document processing with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Textract
all_items.summary: Through a strategic partnership with the AWS Generative AI Innovation Center (GenAIIC), Rocket Close developed an intelligent document processing solution that has significantly reduced processing time, making the process 15 times faster. The solution, which uses Amazon Textract for OCR processing and Amazon Bedrock for foundation models (FMs), achieves a strong 90% overall accuracy in document segmentation, classification, and field extraction.

all_items.title: Persist session state with filesystem configuration and execute shell commands
all_items.summary: In this post, we go through how to use managed session storage to persist your agent's filesystem state and how to execute shell commands directly in your agent's environment.

all_items.title: Quoting Willy Tarreau
all_items.summary: On the kernel security list we've seen a huge bump of reports. We were between 2 and 3 per week maybe two years ago, then reached probably 10 a week over the last year with the only difference being only AI slop, and now since the beginning of the year we're around 5-10 per day depending on the days (fridays and tuesdays seem the worst). Now

all_items.title: Quoting Daniel Stenberg
all_items.summary:
The challenge with AI in open source security has transitioned from an AI slop tsunami into more of a ... plain security report tsunami. Less slop but lots of reports. Many of them really good.

I'm spending hours per day on this now. It's intense.

—
